<details>
<summary> code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'dart:io';

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:webview_flutter/webview_flutter.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();

    // enable hybrid composition
    if (Platform.isAndroid) WebView.platform = SurfaceAndroidWebView();
  }

  final String _html = """
  <!DOCTYPE html>
  <html>
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
    <meta name="viewport"
        content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no">
    <title>Amaze UI</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="http://cdn.amazeui.org/amazeui/2.5.0/css/amazeui.min.css"/>
  </head>
  <body>
    <input type="file" />
  <script src="http://code.jquery.com/jquery-2.1.4.min.js"></script>
  <script src="http://cdn.amazeui.org/amazeui/2.5.0/js/amazeui.min.js"></script>
  </body> 
  </html>
  """;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: WebView(
        initialUrl: 'flutter.dev',
        onWebViewCreated: (WebViewController controller) {
          controller.loadUrl(
            Uri.dataFromString(_html, mimeType: 'text/html').toString(),
          );
        },
      ),
    );
  }
}

```
</details>

As I see, it would not allow to specify file choosing options as only camera or only gallery, it will always use both of them. I think it's important feature to be able to specify that. I would imagine a callback (`List<AllowedInputTypes> onFileChoose()`) or a setting to controll that.

> Hey, I have a workaround for this. This will take another plugin though. 😄 The plugin: https://github.com/fluttercommunity/flutter_webview_plugin The code:
> 
> ```
>         child: WebviewScaffold(
>           url: _url,
>           withLocalStorage: true,
>         ),
>       ),
> ```

How to use this plugin

**Update:**

We are currently working on [moving the platform channel boundary closer to native APIs](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/93732) and [updating the standard platform interface structure](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/94051). Work on this issue will most likely follow sometime after these have been finished.
I tried this package [https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_webview_pro](flutter_webview_pro) and it worked well on android for me.

Work on this issue will most likely follow sometime after these have been finished.

And based on discussion in https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/3225, the way we'll proceed here is to provide a way to register a handler for the callback on the Dart side, and we'll likely provide an example (and if there's enough need, potentially a default implementation) of how to implement that call using existing plugins.

@FirdousNath Please file a new issue showing your `onShowFileChooser` handling and providing details about what isn't working.

## Semantics object support for edge triggered semantics

Framework support for flutter/flutter#13704.  Also requires https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/4901 in the engine first.


* Adds a `route: bool` parameter to Semantics, SemanticsProperties which corresponds to the SemanticsFlag.isRoute
 * Adds a route + explicitChildren semantic to cupertino and mv routes
 * Adds a route semantic to drawer
 * Adds a route semantic to the AlertDialog
  
Also updates a significant number of tests which asserted a specific semantics structure.
Can you please also add a test to ensure that the routeName given to a Semantics widget ends up on the appropriate semantics node?
I've updated the PR with the new route behavior, as well as all of the tests which this broke
Doesn't appear to be any difference between null and empty string - might have to do with how tonic encodes/decodes

I've updated the navigator to take a parameter `typedef String RouteAccessibilityNameCallback(String routeName, bool isInitialRoute)`.  The default value handles splitting strings on "/", the default initial route name of "home", and iOS specific logic.  This information is computed when a route is pushed and placed into RouteSettings.  The material page then uses this info to construct a semantics node

Another ... hiccup.  The platform specific logic for iOS changes between Dialogs (announced) and Pages/Drawers (chime).  In that case, it might make more sense to introduce `RouteName`, `DialogName` and `DrawerName` widgets for semantics information if this logic is to be handled at the framework/widget level.  But then, instead of doing all of that - why not just add a named arguments to all dialogs and drawers so that we can have a default semantic value.  RouteName could stay as a widget since routes aren't constructed in such a way where we have a clear root widget.

Which means that instead of pushing this off into another PR I should just rip off the bandaid now
@goderbauer , @Hixie  I've updated this PR to work with the engine PR.  Still have not updated tests - this will be a mostly mechanical but time consuming effort
I've updated this PR with the default localized semantics labels, and cleaned up the documentation to reduce repetition - an explanation of scopesRoute and namesRoute now points to dart:ui.

I've updated the PR to remove the routeName widget.  until we have a more general way to create semantics nodes which are unfocusable, I don't think it can be implemented in a clear way.  At any rate, it is not necessary to get route semantics working

## Bare bones widget inspector support.

Sent out for review without tests in expectation of significant changes based on code review feedback.

Toggle the widget inspector from the flutter tool by pressing 'i'.
When the widget inspector is select mode:
Pointer down to to inspect a widget.
Pointer click to finalize selection of a widget. You can now interact
with the application as you normally would but with the inspected widget
highlighted.
Click the inspect icon in bottom left corner of screen to reactivate
select mode.
That error from the CLA bot typically means the email address you set up for git (and got used in the git commit) is not associated with your github account.
looks like you just want to set `git config --global user.email`

The general approach seems fine. One major problem is that there's no way to know when the item you are watching is moving, so if you inspect something that's animating, your border won't move with it. To fix that you'll need something like https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/3861 (though I don't really know how you'd handle it even with that exactly). You could work around it by somehow requesting that you paint every frame that paints, but the system basically spends all its effort making that impossible because it'd be a performance disaster, so there's no good API to hook into to do that right now either.

It sounds like what you want to do is actually hook into the pipeline at a pretty low level. If you're ok with requiring that people add a line of code to their main.dart and import a separate package, you could provide a new binding that overrides the FlutterWidgetsBinding to provide the relevant hooks, in much the same way that the test framework does that. (In your case, you'd probably need to create a new BuildOwner so that you could override the handling of dirty widgets so that any time you have a dirty widget, you also mark the inspector as potentially needing an update, as well as creating a new PipelineOwner so you could catch any changes that result in a paint update, in case any of the render objects are repainting independent of any widget updates.)

The alternative would be to hook onDrawFrame to just always mark the inspector dirty, passing through every call to the underlying binding. That would be really sketchy though. For example, it would totally break down if you had multiple inspector widgets at once. It would also be less efficient, though honestly even the binding override won't be great in that respect.
@jacob314 Should we close this PR for now?

@jacob314 I'm going to close this PR since my understanding is that you're not awaiting review and do not plan to land this exact PR, and so we should take it off the review queue. Don't hesitate to reopen it or open a new one when you're ready for another round of review!
@jacob314 You reopened the PR, does that mean you want review again?
I'm going through the comments again now. I need to address a few more of them before it is ready to be reviewed again.
